channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: Iran war threatens jet fuel shortage that could disrupt summer travel in Asia and Europe
item.description: Oil executives have warned that the supply disruption in the Middle East will soon cause actual fuel shortages in some parts of the world.

item.title: Shell tops profit estimates as Iran war boosts oil price, cuts share buybacks
item.description: Britain's Shell posted stronger-than-expected quarterly profit as the Iran war sent fossil fuel prices soaring.

item.title: It’s not just Big Oil. Wind giants welcome profit beats as Iran war spurs energy pivot
item.description: Norway's Equinor told CNBC that the company expects the Iran war to deliver a boost to its transition industries.

item.title: Snap issues cautious guidance as Perplexity deal ends, Middle East 'geopolitical situation' causes uncertainty
item.description: Snap reported first-quarter earnings and provided cautious sales guidance while revealing it no longer has a deal with the generative AI startup Perplexity.

item.title: The incredible chip sector rally is leaving behind its most notable stock. What's wrong with Nvidia?
item.description: The GPU-maker is flat since April 27, while Intel and Micron have popped more than 30% in that time.

item.title: Oil rises as U.S.-Iran tensions keep traders focused on Strait of Hormuz risks
item.description: Oil prices rose Thursday, as investors continue to assess the latest developments in the Middle East amid concerns over renewed tensions between Iran and the U.S.

item.title: SoftBank shares surge over 16% as Japan tech-fueled rally lifts Nikkei 225 to record highs
item.description: The benchmark Nikkei 225 surged over 5% to a record high as Japan reopened after extended Golden Week holidays.

item.title: Apollo CEO Rowan warns of market correction, slams 'egregious' practices at rival insurers
item.description: Apollo CEO Marc Rowan warned that markets face an elevated risk of unexpected shocks, saying he is positioning the firm defensively for an expected correction.

item.title: Asian Development Bank's $70 billion energy and digital infra push puts Southeast Asia center stage
item.description: Southeast Asia to benefit significantly from Asian Development Bank's $70 billion new energy and digital infrastructure plan in Asia and the Pacific by 2035.

item.title: Japan's Nikkei 225 tops 62,000 for the first time as Asia markets look past Trump’s Iran threats
item.description: Asia-Pacific markets jumped, shrugging off renewed tensions in the Middle East.

item.title: From jewelers to health tech, CEOs want tariff refunds as earnings take a hit
item.description: Philips and Pandora on Wednesday announced their intentions to apply for tariff rebates in the aftermath of President Trump's "liberation day" blitz.

item.title: Inside India newsletter: Social media is reshaping the world's fastest-growing market for global brands
item.description: Social media influencers are urging consumers to read labels and pushing traditional companies to make healthier products or risk being replaced by newer entrants.

item.title: Nvidia to invest up to $3.2 billion in Corning as part of massive optical fiber deal with 3 new factories focused on AI
item.description: Corning is opening three new advanced manufacturing plants in the U.S. dedicated entirely to optical technologies for Nvidia.

item.title: Ted Turner, outspoken founder of CNN, dies at 87
item.description: Ted Turner, the brash sportsman and entrepreneur whose ambition and instincts led to a media empire that included groundbreaking news network CNN, has died.

item.title: Jim Cramer says Big Tech cannot afford to be cheap on AI spending
item.description: Cloud computing giants cannot afford to pinch pennies on the artificial intelligence buildout, CNBC's Jim Cramer argued Wednesday.

item.title: Anthropic CEO says 80-fold growth in first quarter explains 'difficulties with compute'
item.description: At Anthropic's developer conference in San Francisco, CEO Dario Amodei said the company is 'working as quickly as possible' to provide additional compute.

item.title: DoorDash pops 12% on strong earnings, upbeat order growth guidance
item.description: DoorDash is in the middle of a massive spending initiative as it builds out a new tech platform following a string of acquisitions.

item.title: Berkshire-owned distribution giant to deploy driverless big rigs across U.S. Sun Belt
item.description: Berkshire Hathaway-owned McLane, one of America's biggest distribution companies, is deploying self-driving big rigs with Aurora for its restaurants business.

item.title: Arm's quarter shows how it's carving a lucrative path in the crowded CPU resurgence
item.description: This central processing unit renaissance was confirmed when Intel reported earnings two weeks ago. AMD and now Arm told similar stories.

item.title: Warner Bros. Discovery books $2.9 billion net loss tied to Paramount deal, restructuring costs
item.description: Paramount agreed to pay a Netflix termination fee, but the cost lives on WBD's books until the close of the deal.

item.title: AMD's Su explains what's behind massive forecast change as stock roars 19% on earnings
item.description: CEO Lisa Su highlighted the growing demand for central processing units, doubling her long-term forecast after strong first-quarter earnings.

item.title: Ending soon: All-time high Marriott card bonus offers worth up to 200,000 points
item.description: These Marriott Bonvoy credit card welcome bonuses are at all-time highs, but they won't be for much longer.

item.title: Thursday's big stock stories: What’s likely to move the market in the next trading session
item.description: The S&P 500 hit a new all-time high and closed above 7,300 for the first time in Wednesday's session as traders grew optimistic about a deal in Iran.

item.title: Will Elon Musk win his lawsuit against Sam Altman's OpenAI? Here's what prediction markets think
item.description: Trading activity on Elon Musk's suit against OpenAI is the second highest out of all prediction market contracts related to the billionaire.

item.title: FanDuel CEO Amy Howe is out after five years at the sportsbook
item.description: Howe has overseen FanDuel during a time of dramatic expansion in sports gambling and as prediction markets have exploded in popularity.

item.title: Elon Musk's Terafab chip factory in Texas could cost up to $119 billion, filing shows
item.description: Elon Musk's giant Terafab facility in Texas could cost up to $119 billion, with hopes of manufacturing chips for Tesla, SpaceX and xAI

item.title: Anthropic, SpaceX announce compute deal that includes space development
item.description: Anthropic has signed a deal with SpaceX, which owns rival xAI, to use all of the compute capacity at the company’s Colossus 1 data center in Memphis, Tennessee.

item.title: Goodbye quarterly earnings? Here's when traders believe this big change will happen
item.description: Traders are evenly divided as to whether the change will happen by Jan. 1. That would be an unusually fast timeline for the commission.

item.title: Marco Rubio heads to the Vatican as 2028 presidential buzz ramps up
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio is a frontrunner to succeed President Donald Trump in 2028, and Trump is sending him to meet with Pope Leo XIV.

item.title: Trump says Iran will be bombed at a 'much higher level' if it doesn't agree to peace deal
item.description: Stocks rose and oil prices plunged on news reports signaling the U.S. and Iran could soon strike an agreement to end the war.
